docs.title: cryptovol-python
docs.text:
The official Python SDK for the CryptoVol API — institutional-grade implied volatility data for crypto options.

What's included

  • SABR-calibrated vol surfaces — arbitrage-free interpolation
  • 3 sessions/day — Asia, London, US closes
  • 6 assets — BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, AVAX, TRX
  • Constant-maturity history for clean backtesting
  • Greeks computed at any (K, T) point

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install cryptovol

For pandas integration:

pip install "cryptovol[pandas]"

docs.title: A 30-second tour
docs.text:
from cryptovol import CryptoVol

cv = CryptoVol(api_key="YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY")

# Daily IV index
idx = cv.vol_index(ccy="BTC", tenor="30D")

# Single vol point with Greeks
pt = cv.vol_surface(
    ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="C",
    include_analytics=True,
)
print(pt.vol, pt.analytics.greeks.delta)

# Many points at once
resp = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": m}
        for m in [0.9, 1.0, 1.1]
    ],
)

# Constant-maturity history
hist = cv.vol_history(ccy="BTC", tenor="1M",
                      strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0)

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Quick start — full walkthrough of every endpoint
  • Recipes — common analytics patterns
  • Error handling — exception hierarchy and retry behavior
  • API reference — every method, parameter, and return type

docs.title: Get a key
docs.text: Sign up at cryptovol.io/api — BASIC, PRO, and ULTRA tiers available.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text: Every API failure becomes a typed Python exception. Catch the base class to handle anything, or a specific subclass to react narrowly.

docs.title: Hierarchy
docs.text:
CryptoVolError                  # base — catch this to handle anything
├── AuthenticationError         # 401, or 403 without "plan" in the body
├── PlanLimitError              # 403 — tier blocks asset/session/history/Greeks
├── NotFoundError               # 404 — no data for that date/session
├── ValidationError             # 400 / 422 — malformed params
├── RateLimitError              # 429 — quota exceeded
├── ServerError                 # 5xx — transient server-side failure
└── TimeoutError                # request exceeded `timeout`

Every exception carries:

  • e.message — human-readable text from the server
  • e.status_code — HTTP status code (when applicable)
  • e.response_body — the parsed JSON body (or raw text) for debugging

docs.title: Catch-all
docs.text:
from cryptovol import CryptoVol, CryptoVolError

try:
    pt = cv.vol_surface(ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
                        strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0)
except CryptoVolError as e:
    log.error(f"CryptoVol call failed [{e.status_code}]: {e.message}")

docs.title: React to plan limits
docs.text:
If you ship code that might run under different subscription tiers, fall back gracefully:

from cryptovol import PlanLimitError

try:
    pt = cv.vol_surface(ccy="ETH", expiry="2026-12-26",
                        strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0,
                        session="asia")
except PlanLimitError:
    # Asia session is ULTRA-only — fall back to US
    pt = cv.vol_surface(ccy="ETH", expiry="2026-12-26",
                        strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0,
                        session="us")

docs.title: Distinguish auth vs plan-limit
docs.text:
Both come back as HTTP 403. The SDK heuristic: if the response body mentions "plan", "upgrade", or "tier", you get PlanLimitError; otherwise AuthenticationError.

from cryptovol import AuthenticationError, PlanLimitError

try:
    ...
except AuthenticationError:
    print("Check your API key.")
except PlanLimitError as e:
    print(f"Upgrade needed: {e}")

docs.title: Retries
docs.text:
The client retries transient failures (5xx and 429) automatically with exponential backoff. To customize:

cv = CryptoVol(api_key="...", max_retries=5)   # default is 3
cv = CryptoVol(api_key="...", max_retries=0)   # disable retries entirely

RateLimitError honors the Retry-After header when the server sends one.

docs.title: Get an API key
docs.text:
Sign up at cryptovol.io/api. You'll get a RapidAPI key — use it as the api_key parameter when constructing the client.

from cryptovol import CryptoVol

cv = CryptoVol(api_key="YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY")

Keep your key out of source control

Load it from an environment variable instead:

import os
cv = CryptoVol(api_key=os.environ["CRYPTOVOL_API_KEY"])

docs.title: 1. Vol index — the simplest call
docs.text:
vol_index returns a daily constant-maturity IV time series. Think VIX, but for crypto.

idx = cv.vol_index(ccy="BTC", tenor="30D")

print(f"{idx.count} daily observations")
for pt in idx.data[-5:]:
    print(f"  {pt.date}: {pt.vol:.2f}%")

With pandas (pip install "cryptovol[pandas]"):

df = cv.vol_index(ccy="BTC", tenor="30D",
                  start_date="2025-01-01").to_dataframe()
df["vol"].plot()

docs.title: 2. Vol surface — single point
docs.text:
vol_surface returns one SABR-interpolated point. Pick a strike convention that fits your workflow:

MoneynessDeltaAbsolute 
pt = cv.vol_surface(
    ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
    strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0,  # ATM
)
print(pt.vol)

pt = cv.vol_surface(
    ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="C",
)
# SDK has used SABR-iterative inversion to find K such that BS-delta = 0.25
print(pt.strike, pt.vol)

pt = cv.vol_surface(
    ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
    strike_type="strike", strike_value=100_000,
)

docs.title: Adding Greeks
docs.text:
Set include_analytics=True (PRO/ULTRA plans only):

pt = cv.vol_surface(
    ccy="BTC", expiry="2026-12-26",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="C",
    include_analytics=True,
)

g = pt.analytics.greeks
print(f"Δ={g.delta:.4f}  Γ={g.gamma:.6f}  V={g.vega:.2f}  Θ={g.theta:.2f}")

docs.title: 3. Vol surface (bulk) — many points at once
docs.text:
When you need a smile, a term structure, or a grid, use the bulk endpoint instead of looping over vol_surface. The API loads the snapshot once and reuses it for every query.

resp = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": m}
        for m in [0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 1.0, 1.05, 1.1, 1.2]
    ],
)

for pt in resp.successful:
    print(f"{pt.strike_value:.2f}x  IV={pt.vol:.2f}%")

Individual failures don't fail the request — check .successful and .failed:

if resp.failed:
    for f in resp.failed:
        print(f"  {f.expiry}: {f.error}")

docs.title: 4. Vol history — historical constant-maturity series
docs.text:
vol_history is purpose-built for backtests: a clean daily series at a fixed maturity and fixed strike. Available on every plan — the history window scales by tier (BASIC = 30 days, PRO = 1 year, ULTRA = full archive).

hist = cv.vol_history(
    ccy="BTC", tenor="1M",
    strike_type="moneyness", strike_value=1.0,  # ATM
    start_date="2025-01-01",
)
print(f"{hist.count} daily ATM IV observations")

For delta strikes, supply option_type:

hist_25c = cv.vol_history(
    ccy="BTC", tenor="1M",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="C",
)

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text: Concrete patterns for the analyses people actually run against the CryptoVol API.

docs.title: Plot the 30-day BTC IV index
docs.text:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from cryptovol import CryptoVol

cv = CryptoVol(api_key="...")
df = cv.vol_index(ccy="BTC", tenor="30D",
                  start_date="2025-01-01").to_dataframe()

df["vol"].plot(figsize=(10, 4), title="BTC 30D Implied Volatility")
plt.ylabel("IV (%)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

docs.title: Reconstruct a vol smile
docs.text:
EXPIRY = "2026-12-26"
GRID = [0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 1.0, 1.05, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3]

resp = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {"expiry": EXPIRY, "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": m}
        for m in GRID
    ],
)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
xs = [p.strike_value for p in resp.successful]
ys = [p.vol           for p in resp.successful]
plt.plot(xs, ys, marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Moneyness (K/S)"); plt.ylabel("IV (%)")
plt.title(f"BTC smile — {EXPIRY}")
plt.show()

docs.title: Term structure of ATM vol
docs.text:
EXPIRIES = ["2026-06-26", "2026-09-26", "2026-12-26", "2027-03-26"]

resp = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {"expiry": e, "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": 1.0}
        for e in EXPIRIES
    ],
)

for pt in resp.successful:
    print(f"{pt.expiry}: ATM IV = {pt.vol:.2f}%")

docs.title: 25-delta risk-reversal time series
docs.text:
The 25-delta risk reversal (25Δ call IV minus 25Δ put IV) is a standard skew measure. Positive = call-skewed, negative = put-skewed.

call = cv.vol_history(
    ccy="BTC", tenor="1M",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="C",
).to_dataframe()

put = cv.vol_history(
    ccy="BTC", tenor="1M",
    strike_type="delta", strike_value=0.25, option_type="P",
).to_dataframe()

rr = (call["vol"] - put["vol"]).rename("rr_25d_1m")
rr.plot(title="BTC 1M 25Δ Risk Reversal")

docs.title: Butterfly term structure
docs.text:
A standard fly is (call_iv + put_iv) / 2 - atm_iv.

call_25 = cv.vol_history(ccy="BTC", tenor="1M", strike_type="delta",
                         strike_value=0.25, option_type="C").to_dataframe()
put_25  = cv.vol_history(ccy="BTC", tenor="1M", strike_type="delta",
                         strike_value=0.25, option_type="P").to_dataframe()
atm     = cv.vol_history(ccy="BTC", tenor="1M", strike_type="moneyness",
                         strike_value=1.0).to_dataframe()

fly = ((call_25["vol"] + put_25["vol"]) / 2 - atm["vol"]).rename("fly_25d_1m")

docs.title: Bulk Greeks for portfolio risk
docs.text:
positions = [
    {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_value": 0.9, "option_type": "P"},
    {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_value": 1.0, "option_type": "C"},
    {"expiry": "2026-12-26", "strike_value": 1.1, "option_type": "C"},
]

resp = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {
            "expiry": p["expiry"],
            "strike_type": "moneyness",
            "strike_value": p["strike_value"],
            "option_type": p["option_type"],
            "include_analytics": True,
        }
        for p in positions
    ],
)

portfolio_delta = sum(r.analytics.greeks.delta for r in resp.successful)
portfolio_vega  = sum(r.analytics.greeks.vega  for r in resp.successful)
print(f"Δ={portfolio_delta:+.3f}  ν={portfolio_vega:+.1f}")

docs.title: API reference
docs.text: Auto-generated from docstrings. Every public class and method is here.

docs.title: CryptoVol
docs.text:
Synchronous client for the CryptoVol REST API.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default api_key str

Your CryptoVol API key (the cvk_live_... value shown on https://www.cryptovol.io/account). Sent as the X-CryptoVol-Key header on every request.

required base_url str

Override the API base URL. Defaults to the production CryptoVol API.

DEFAULT_BASE_URL timeout float

Per-request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 30s.

DEFAULT_TIMEOUT max_retries int

How many times to retry transient failures (network errors and 5xx responses) with exponential backoff. Set to 0 to disable.

DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES user_agent str

Custom User-Agent header. Useful if you're embedding the SDK in your own product and want clean attribution in server logs.

DEFAULT_USER_AGENT

docs.title: close
docs.text:
close() -> None

Close the underlying HTTP client. Safe to call multiple times.

docs.title: vol_index
docs.text:
vol_index(ccy: str = 'BTC', tenor: str = '30D', start_date: Optional[str] = None, end_date: Optional[str] = None, *, raw: bool = False) -> Union[VolIndexResponse, Dict[str, Any]]

Daily implied volatility index (CryptoVIX-style time series).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol — one of BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, AVAX, TRX. Subject to your plan tier.

'BTC' tenor str

Constant-maturity tenor (e.g. 7D, 30D, 1M, 3M).

'30D' start_date Optional[str]

Optional YYYY-MM-DD bounds. If omitted, defaults to roughly the last year (or the most that your plan allows).

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False

docs.title: vol_surface
docs.text:
vol_surface(ccy: str, expiry: str, strike_type: StrikeType, strike_value: float, *, option_type: OptionType = 'C', session: Session = 'us', date: Optional[str] = None, include_analytics: bool = False, raw: bool = False) -> Union[VolSurfacePoint, Dict[str, Any]]

Single-point SABR-interpolated implied vol query.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol.

required expiry str

Option expiry date, YYYY-MM-DD. Must be in the future relative to the snapshot date.

required strike_type StrikeType

How strike_value is interpreted:

  • "strike" — absolute strike price (e.g. 100_000)
  • "moneyness" — K/S ratio (e.g. 1.0 for ATM, 0.9 for 10% OTM put)
  • "delta" — signed/unsigned BS delta magnitude (e.g. 0.25)
required strike_value float

The numeric strike, moneyness, or delta — see strike_type.

required option_type OptionType

"C" (call, default) or "P" (put). Required when strike_type="delta"; ignored otherwise.

'C' session Session

"asia", "london", or "us". Subject to your plan tier.

'us' date Optional[str]

Optional snapshot date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the latest available snapshot.

None include_analytics bool

If True, also return spot, forward, yield rate, BS price, and Greeks. Requires a PRO or ULTRA plan.

False raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False

docs.title: vol_surface_bulk
docs.text:
vol_surface_bulk(ccy: str, queries: List[Dict[str, Any]], *, session: Session = 'us', date: Optional[str] = None, raw: bool = False) -> Union[BulkVolResponse, Dict[str, Any]]

Resolve many (expiry, strike) points in a single request.

Far more efficient than looping over :meth:vol_surface — one snapshot is loaded once and reused for every query.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol.

required queries List[Dict[str, Any]]

List of dicts, each with keys:

  • expiry (str, required) — YYYY-MM-DD
  • strike_type (str, required) — "strike", "moneyness", or "delta"
  • strike_value (float, required)
  • option_type (str, optional) — "C" or "P" (default "C")
  • include_analytics (bool, optional) — default False
required session Session

"asia", "london", or "us".

'us' date Optional[str]

Optional snapshot date.

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False Example 
response = cv.vol_surface_bulk(
    ccy="BTC",
    queries=[
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": 0.9},
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": 1.0},
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "moneyness", "strike_value": 1.1},
        {"expiry": "2026-09-26", "strike_type": "delta",
         "strike_value": 0.25, "option_type": "C", "include_analytics": True},
    ],
)
for item in response.successful:
    print(item.expiry, item.strike, item.vol)

docs.title: vol_surface_raw
docs.text:
vol_surface_raw(ccy: str, *, session: Session = 'us', date: Optional[str] = None, raw: bool = False) -> Union[RawVolSurface, Dict[str, Any]]

Raw market-quoted vol surface for one snapshot (PRO and ULTRA).

Returns every listed expiry with its quoted strike ladder (strikes) and the market implied vol at each strike (mkt_vol, index-aligned), plus forward, atm_strike, and spot. This is the discrete market data before model fitting — for a smooth interpolated vol at an arbitrary strike/moneyness/delta use :meth:vol_surface or :meth:vol_surface_bulk.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol.

required session Session

"asia", "london", or "us". Subject to your plan tier.

'us' date Optional[str]

Optional snapshot date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the latest available snapshot.

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False Example 
surf = cv.vol_surface_raw(ccy="BTC", session="us")
for exp in surf.expiries:
    print(exp.expiry, exp.forward, len(exp.strikes), "strikes")

docs.title: vol_history
docs.text:
vol_history(ccy: str = 'BTC', tenor: str = '1M', strike_type: Literal['moneyness', 'delta'] = 'moneyness', strike_value: float = 1.0, *, option_type: OptionType = 'C', session: Session = 'us', start_date: Optional[str] = None, end_date: Optional[str] = None, raw: bool = False) -> Union[VolHistoryResponse, Dict[str, Any]]

Historical time series at a constant maturity and constant strike.

Ideal for backtesting, regression analysis, and vol regime monitoring.

Available on every plan. The history window scales by tier: BASIC = last 30 days, PRO = last 1 year, ULTRA = full archive. BASIC and PRO are limited to the US session; ULTRA gets all three.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol.

'BTC' tenor str

Constant-maturity tenor: 1D, 1W, 2W, 3W, 1M, 2M, 3M, 6M, 9M, or 1Y.

'1M' strike_type Literal['moneyness', 'delta']

"moneyness" (K/S) or "delta". Absolute strikes are not supported for history queries.

'moneyness' strike_value float

Must be a value on the published grid — see the API docs for the full list. Common moneyness points: 0.9, 0.95, 1.0, 1.05, 1.1. Common deltas: 0.10, 0.25.

1.0 option_type OptionType

"C" or "P". Required when strike_type="delta".

'C' session Session

"asia" (05:00 UTC), "london" (12:00 UTC), or "us" (16:00 UTC, default). asia/london require ULTRA.

'us' start_date Optional[str]

YYYY-MM-DD bounds. Bounded by your plan's history window.

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False

docs.title: spot_history
docs.text:
spot_history(ccy: str = 'BTC', *, session: Session = 'us', start_date: Optional[str] = None, end_date: Optional[str] = None, raw: bool = False) -> Union[SpotHistoryResponse, Dict[str, Any]]

Daily spot price time series per session snapshot.

Available on every plan. The history window scales by tier: BASIC = last 30 days, PRO = last 1 year, ULTRA = full archive. BASIC and PRO are limited to the US session; ULTRA gets all three.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol. Subject to your plan tier.

'BTC' session Session

"asia", "london", or "us" (default). asia/london require ULTRA.

'us' start_date Optional[str]

Optional YYYY-MM-DD bounds. Defaults to ~1 year (clamped by plan).

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False Example 
spots = cv.spot_history(ccy="BTC", start_date="2026-04-01")
for pt in spots.data[-5:]:
    print(pt.date, pt.spot)

docs.title: realized_vol
docs.text:
realized_vol(ccy: str = 'BTC', window: int = 30, *, session: Session = 'us', start_date: Optional[str] = None, end_date: Optional[str] = None, raw: bool = False) -> Union[RealizedVolResponse, Dict[str, Any]]

Rolling annualized realized volatility from daily spot log-returns.

RV is reported in percent (e.g. 38.5 means 38.5%). Annualization factor is √365 (crypto markets trade 24/7). The first window days are dropped (insufficient history for the rolling stdev).

Same plan limits as :meth:spot_history: BASIC = 30 days, PRO = 1 year, ULTRA = full archive. BASIC/PRO US-only; ULTRA all sessions.

Tip: the API computes RV from spot only within the requested date range — it does not auto-extend lookback by window days. To get RV at date D, include at least window prior days in start_date.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ccy str

Asset symbol.

'BTC' window int

Rolling window in days (2–365). Default 30.

30 session Session

"asia", "london", or "us" (default).

'us' start_date Optional[str]

Optional YYYY-MM-DD bounds.

None raw bool

If True, return the parsed JSON dict instead of a typed model.

False Example 
rv = cv.realized_vol(ccy="BTC", window=30)
print(f"30d RV: {rv.data[-1].rv:.2f}%")

docs.title: CryptoVolError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base exception for all errors raised by the CryptoVol SDK.

docs.title: AuthenticationError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 401 / 403 — invalid or missing API key, or non-allowed origin.

docs.title: PlanLimitError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 403 when the request hits a plan-tier limit.

Examples: querying ETH on a BASIC plan, requesting Greeks without PRO, querying dates older than your plan's history window.

The server's error message typically tells you which tier unlocks the feature.

docs.title: NotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 404 — no data available for the requested asset/date/session.

docs.title: ValidationError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 400 / 422 — malformed parameters (bad ccy, bad date format, etc.).

docs.title: RateLimitError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 429 — daily/monthly quota exceeded.

docs.title: ServerError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised on 5xx — transient server-side failure.

docs.title: TimeoutError
docs.text:
Bases: CryptoVolError

Raised when a request exceeds the configured timeout.
